2604.12019.title: A longitudinal health agent framework
2604.12019.paper_summary: Although artificial intelligence (AI) agents are increasingly proposed to support potentially longitudinal health tasks, such as symptom management, behavior change, and patient support, most current implementations fall short of facilitating user intent and fostering accountability. This contrasts with prior work on supporting longitudinal needs, where follow-up, coherent reasoning, and sustained alignment with individuals' goals are critical for both effectiveness and safety. In this paper, we draw on established clinical and personal health informatics frameworks to define what it would mean to orchestrate longitudinal health interactions with AI agents. We propose a multi-layer framework and corresponding agent architecture that operationalizes adaptation, coherence, continuity, and agency across repeated interactions. Through representative use cases, we demonstrate how longitudinal agents can maintain meaningful engagement, adapt to evolving goals, and support safe, personalized decision-making over time. Our findings underscore both the promise and the complexity of designing systems capable of supporting health trajectories beyond isolated interactions, and we offer guidance for future research and development in multi-session, user-centered health AI.
2604.12019.paper_summary_zh: 雖然人工智慧（AI）代理人越來越多地被提議用來支持潛在的長期健康任務，例如症狀管理、行為改變和病人支持，但目前大多數實施仍未能促進用戶意圖並培養責任感。這與先前的研究形成對比，先前的研究在支持長期需求方面，跟進、一致的推理和與個人目標的持續對齊對於有效性和安全性至關重要。在本文中，我們借鑒已建立的臨床和個人健康資訊學框架，定義如何協調與AI代理人的長期健康互動。我們提出了一個多層框架和相應的代理架構，將適應性、一致性、連續性和主體性在重複互動中具體化。通過代表性的使用案例，我們展示了長期代理人如何保持有意義的參與，適應不斷演變的目標，並支持安全、個性化的決策制定。我們的研究結果強調了設計能夠支持超越孤立互動的健康軌跡系統的潛力和複雜性，並為未來在多會話、以用戶為中心的健康AI方面的研究和開發提供了指導。
2604.12019.author: Georgianna "Blue" Lin et.al.
2604.12019.authors: Georgianna "Blue" Lin, Rencong Jiang, Noémie Elhadad, Xuhai "Orson" Xu
